title: Tell Qadesh: The Connected Dig Plan
strategies: best single cell: dig only the highest-yield cell as a size-1 trench (reproduces the evaluator's weak reference, ~0.1)
single-seed region growing: start at the best cell and greedily add the boundary neighbour that most improves plan value until no addition helps (captures one hotspot, never removes or re-seeds)

multi-start add/remove hill-climbing with seeded random kicks: grow from every top hotspot cell, absorb profitable cells and trim cells whose shoring outweighs their yield (connectivity-preserving removals), and bridge two hotspots when the bridge pays -- a real search that beats single-seed growing
stronger metaheuristics: simulated annealing / tabu over connected regions, exact graph-cut relaxation followed by connectivity repair, or beam search over region boundaries
